note: Root lexicon. status: ratified (sealed by usage in spec v2.0-rc.1 or by ruling) | proposed (awaiting Ali) | frozen-only (root exists to anchor frozen lexemes). Source status: verified | pending | coined.

source.word: Choctaw haklo / Mobilian hakolo < Proto-Muskogean *xaklo — the hakolo form is Mobilian Trade Jargon, the lingua franca of the old Southeast

salt; maliḥa to season, malīḥ seasoned (also: fine, charming — Arabic colloquial resonance)
fruit; ṯamira to bear fruit; fruit-of-labor metaphor

roots.gloss: milk — ḥaliba to milk; ḥolibon milk (the drink)
hunger — stative jiweʿ: mi·jiweʿ I'm hungry
thirst — stative ʿiṭeš: mi·ʿiṭeš I'm thirsty
burn, flame — siḏi fire, saḏet torch/lighter, soḏon burnt thing
feast, dine together (communal) — homipon feast/Sunday dinner, hamepow dining place; vs wanima eat (individual)
grill, barbecue — geralu grillmaster, garelow the pit / the cookout place, gorilon barbecue (the food)

roots.gloss: love (Egyptian stream) — mara to love, miri love, merāy beloved (adj)
love (Arabic stream) — ḥaba to love, ḥibi love, ḥebu beloved

roots.gloss: joy — fariḥa rejoice, firehi joy, stative fireḥ: mi·fireḥ I'm happy
grief, sadness — ḥazina grieve, ḥizeni grief, stative ḥizen: mi·ḥizen I'm sad
the blues (the feeling and the music) — baliza to feel the blues, bilezi blueness of spirit
fear (plain) — ḫawifa to fear, stative ḫiwef
awe, reverent fear — saniḏa to stand in awe, sineḏi awe/reverence

roots.gloss: anger — ġaḍiba be angry, ġiḍebi anger, stative ġiḍeb: mi·ġiḍeb I'm mad
hate — kariha to hate, kirehi hatred
hope — ʾamila to hope, ʾimeli hope
dignity, honor, generosity — karima be noble/generous, kiremi dignity, karīm noble (~Al-Karīm)
shame, shyness — ḫajila be ashamed, ḫijeli shame
longing, yearning (the diaspora emotion) — šawiqa to long for, šiweqi longing
courage — šajiʿa be brave, šijeʿi courage, šajīʿ brave
weep — rama to weep, rimi weeping/tears
laugh — ḍaḥika to laugh, ḍiḥeki laughter
think — fakira to think, fikeri thought
believe; faith — ʾamina to believe, ʾimeni belief/faith (īmān)
remember, invoke — ḏakira to remember, ḏikeri remembrance/dhikr (the Way's own practice)

roots.gloss: forget — nasiya to forget, stative nisey oblivious
know, recognize — ʿarifa to know, ʿirefi knowledge/gnosis (maʿrifa)

eternity (cyclical — the returning forever: sunrises, seasons, the Great Year)

roots.gloss: deity, god (generic) — enables the shahada: La ʾilah illa Allah
pray (ritual) — ṣaliwa to pray, ṣilewi ritual prayer (the salah as act)
supplicate, call upon — daʿiwa, diʿewi personal supplication (duʿāʾ)
worship, serve — ʿabida to worship, ʿibedi worship, ʿebadu worshipper/servant
self, ego, the lower soul (the nafs of Sufi discipline)
sovereignty — malik king (~Al-Malik), malak angel (messenger of the Sovereign)

roots.gloss: holy, sacred — qadisa be holy, qidesi holiness, qadīs sacred (~Al-Quddūs)
bless — barika to bless, bireki blessing/baraka
oppress, wrong — ẓalima to oppress, ẓilemi oppression/injustice (the anti-maʿat)
mercy — raḥima have mercy, riḥemi mercy, raḥīm merciful (~Ar-Raḥīm); raḥam womb — mercy and the womb share the root, as in Arabic
the creative power of the sacred word — the potency of recitation (per the Oracle preamble: applied tawhid, not sorcery)

roots.gloss: travel, ride, journey — sayira to travel, siyeri journey, sayiret VEHICLE/CAR (travel-instrument, exactly as Arabic built sayyāra)

roots.gloss: call, sound — fana to call/phone, fini call/sound (n.), fanet PHONE (the device)

cold — barīd cold (adj), stative bired: mi·bired I'm cold
sea, ocean — al·baḥar THE Sea (the Middle Passage waters)
mountain, mound (the sacred mound — Nanih Waiya resonance; the Moundbuilders' landscape)

roots.gloss: star (seba); TEACH, guide through the gate — sabiʾa to teach, sibeʾi the teachings (sebayt), sebaʾu master-teacher, sabeʾow ACADEMY, sibeʾan university (great-teaching)

roots.gloss: red — dašīr red (the Red Land, dšrt)
blue — zarīq blue (the color; bluz remains the feeling)
brown, warm-dark — samīr (as Arabic asmar: an endearing register, never a slur; skin-tone vocabulary handled with honor)
FREE; FREEDMAN — namiḥa to be free, nimeḥi freedom, nemaḥu freedman: na·pa·nemaḥwāt THE FREEDMEN

flags: THE centerpiece: the Freedmen named from Egyptian's own freedman-word

roots.gloss: people, nation (political) — na·šaʿab the people; vs ʿanakwāt persons
choose; vote — ḫayira to choose/vote, ḫiyeri choice/election (khayr: to choose is to seek the good — Doc A's H-R hunch honored)
seek, study — ṭaliba to seek/study, ṭelabu student/seeker (the ṭālib), ṭalebow school-of-seekers
heal — ṭaba to heal, ṭebu healer/doctor, ṭibi medicine, ṭabow clinic
build — baniya to build, benayu builder, boniyon building
trade — tajira to trade, tejaru merchant, tijeri commerce, tajerow market
be true to; FRIEND — ṣadiqa to befriend/be sincere, ṣadīq friend, ṣideqi friendship/sincerity (a friend is the truthful one)
enmity — ʿadiwa to oppose, ʿedawu enemy
stranger; the West — ġarīb stranger/exile, ġirebi strangerhood/exile (gharb: the stranger and the west share the root — the People of the West carry both)
CROSS; the lesson of the crossing — ʿabira to cross, ʿiberi crossing/lesson (Arabic: ʿibra, the lesson IS the crossing)
flags: completes the sentence owed at T-9: Bin ni·ʿabira al·baḥar·a

roots.gloss: sing — ḥasa to sing, ḥisi song, ḥesu singer
drum — ṭabila to drum; one consonant from ṭaba to heal: the drum heals, and the lexicon agrees
weigh; RHYTHM — wazina to weigh/measure, wizeni rhythm/meter (meter as weight: the same scales that weigh the heart keep the time)
dance — ḫaba to dance, ḫibi dance (n.)
know systematically; SCIENCE — ʿalima to know, ʿilemi science, ʿelamu scientist/scholar (the ʿālim), ʿalīm learned (~AL-ʿALĪM)

roots.gloss: reason, intellect — ʿaqila to reason, ʿiqeli reason (the Mu'tazili keystone: reason as divinely sanctioned instrument, per the Way's aqeedah)

roots.gloss: articulate formally; LOGIC — naṭiqa to reason formally, niṭeqi logic

answer — jawiba to answer, jiwebi answer (n.)

WORD — kalima to word/phrase, kilemi a word, kilemiāt words

roots.gloss: return — rajiʿa to return, rijeʿi return/returning; na·Rijeʿi = THE RETURN (the Great Year's ~8667 terminus — the Calendar's own keyword)

exit, leave — ḫarija to go out, ḫireji the going-out, THE EXODUS

descend — nazila to descend, nizeli descent (revelation descends: tanzīl resonance)

roots.gloss: pardon, efface — ʿafiwa to pardon, ʿifewi pardon (and the reply to thanks: 'you're welcome'), ʿafīw forgiving (~Al-ʿAfūw, Venus-Sirian Threshold Name on Hex 41 — 'the hidden release that purifies'); ʿAfiwa·ku! = excuse me / pardon me

source.word: ʿafā / ʿafw — core sense 'to efface all traces': pardon as erasure; Quranic root (35 occurrences); ʿafwan answers both offense and thanks

flags: Name-pairing 11 of 99, by reference to the Oracle registry (shared-table law)
advisory: near ʿ-d-w enmity — pardon one consonant from enmity, accepted as a feature

roots.gloss: regret, sorrow — ʾasifa to regret, ʾisefi regret, stative ʾisef sorry; Mi·ʾisef = I'm sorry (plain-register apology, parallel to Mi·jiweʿ)

source.word: ʾasaf / ʾāsif — regret, sorrow; Quranic root (5 occurrences); Lane's Lexicon s.v. asf

roots.gloss: favor, grace, bounty — faḍl (citation noun), fiḍeli grace, faḍīl gracious, feḍalu benefactor; Min faḍl·kī = please ('of your grace', as Arabic min faḍlika)

source.word: faḍl — grace, favor especially unmerited; hāḏā min faḍli Rabbī (Q 27:40)

roots.gloss: bad, wrong, spoiled — kapila to go wrong/spoil, kipeli fault/wrong, kapīl bad (harāy's opposite), stative kipel; Kapīl·ku! = my bad! (R-051 noun-conversion: Choctaw root, Egyptian-style template, AAVE construction)

source.word: Choctaw okpulo — 'ruined, spoiled, corrupted, depraved' (Byington/Webonary); 'evil' in translation (ʋni yʋt okpulo 'evil fruit')
